code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test19-JUN-2020Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, but no hematuria or leukocyturia. Electrolytes are within normal limits except for mildly elevated chloride. Findings are consistent with renal dysfunction, possibly with tubular involvement; recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes such as diabetes or other renal pathology.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
